pattern: The Boyer-Moore voting algorithm cancels one majority vote against one non-majority vote, leaving the candidate with a non-negative count as the winner.
hints: An element appearing more than half the time cannot be completely canceled out — any pair of distinct elements eliminates each other.
Maintain a single candidate and a counter: increment when you see the candidate, decrement otherwise, and replace the candidate whenever the counter hits zero.
After one pass the surviving candidate is the majority element; since the problem guarantees one exists, no verification pass is needed.

edge_inputs.description: single element array — trivially the majority
majority element appears exactly ceil(n/2) times
two-element array where both are equal
majority is a large negative value
majority element does not appear first
alternating non-majority before majority cluster at end

stress.description: large array with majority element planted to exceed n/2 count
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    majority = rng.randint(-10**9, 10**9)
    count = n // 2 + 1
    arr = [majority] * count
    for _ in range(n - count):
        v = rng.randint(-10**9, 10**9)
        while v == majority:
            v = rng.randint(-10**9, 10**9)
        arr.append(v)
    rng.shuffle(arr)
    return [arr]
